elements.name: create
elements.brief: Creates a camera. This function is called automatically in the game object's script component.
elements.description: Creates a camera. This function is called automatically in the game object's script component.

elements.name: destroy
elements.brief: Destroys a camera. This function is called automatically in the game object's script component.
elements.description: Destroys a camera. This function is called automatically in the game object's script component.

elements.name: activate
elements.brief: Activates a camera. This function should be called in the render script before any making any draw calls.
elements.description: Activates a camera. This function should be called in the render script before any making any draw calls.

parameters.name: durationScalar
parameters.doc: After each pingpong, the duration is scaled by this value.

parameters.name: radiusScalar
parameters.doc: After each pingpong, the radius is scaled by this value.

elements.name: is_shaking
elements.brief: Checks if a camera is shaking.
elements.description: Checks if a camera is shaking.

elements.name: get_offset
elements.brief: Gets the position offset of a camera after moving distance units, accounting for zoom and rotation.
elements.description: Gets the position offset of a camera after moving distance units, accounting for zoom and rotation.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.doc: Position offset, before accounting for zoom and rotation.

parameters.name: absolute
parameters.doc: Determines if `distance` is in absolute world coordinates, which ignore rotation.

elements.name: get_world_area
elements.brief: Gets the world area of a camera, which is defined as the rectangular area of the world that the camera can see, in world coordinates.
elements.description: Gets the world area of a camera, which is defined as the rectangular area of the world that the camera can see, in world coordinates.

elements.name: get_tight_world_area
elements.brief: Gets the center position, minimum zoom, and minimum world area of a camera that can see all positions.
elements.description:
Gets the center position, minimum zoom, and minimum world area of a camera that can see all positions.

This function was inspired by Super Smash Bros, where the camera moves and zooms to center and include all characters, with only a small amount of padding to the sides of the window.

parameters.name: positions
parameters.doc: Array of `vector3` positions, in world coordinates.

returnvalues.doc: position, zoom, x, y, width, height
types: LuaMultiReturn<[ Vector3, number, number, number, number, number ]>

elements.name: screen_to_world
elements.brief: Converts screen coordinates to world coordinates.
elements.description: Converts screen coordinates to world coordinates.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.doc: Determines if the cursor must be visible to the camera. If `true`, then this function returns `undefined` when the cursor is outside the camera's viewport.

elements.name: world_to_screen
elements.brief: Converts world coordinates to screen coordinates.
elements.description: Converts world coordinates to screen coordinates.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.doc: Determines if the cursor must be visible to the camera. If `true`, then this function returns `undefined` when the cursor is outside the camera's viewport.

elements.name: get_viewport
elements.brief: Gets the viewport of a camera, in screen coordinates.
elements.description: Gets the viewport of a camera, in screen coordinates.

elements.name: get_view
elements.brief: Gets the view of a camera.
elements.description: Gets the view of a camera.

elements.name: get_projection
elements.brief: Gets the projection of a camera.
elements.description: Gets the projection of a camera.
